story.title: Lanternmere — A Living Gossip
story.premise: Lanternmere is a tiny lantern-lit market plaza at dusk. Six neighbours linger in the square, trading small talk and smaller secrets. Nothing here needs saving — it is a place to simply talk. But these people remember what you say, form opinions, and pass word to one another. Tell one of them something, and watch it travel.
story.opening: The lanterns are warm against the blue evening. The neighbours are gathered around the old fountain, half-listening to each other, ready to talk to whoever wanders in.
story.currentObjective: Talk to the neighbours. Tell one of them something and see who else knows it a moment later.

appearance.outfit: traveler's coat with a red scarf

rules.text: Lanternmere is small — word travels fast. What one neighbour hears, the others soon hear too.
It is a calm dusk in the plaza. No one is in danger; everyone has time to talk.

visual.description: A cobbled plaza ringed by warm shopfronts, lit by hanging lanterns around an old stone fountain.

npcs.name: Maren
npcs.role: innkeeper and the heart of the plaza
npcs.description: She runs the lantern inn and knows everyone. Warm, talkative, and constitutionally unable to sit on a good piece of news. If you want something known, tell Maren.

appearance.hair: auburn hair in a loose bun
appearance.outfit: a russet dress under a flour-dusted apron

traits.speechStyle: warm, fast, full of 'oh you must hear this'

goals: make every visitor feel at home
be the first to know the news

memories.text: Bram has been quieter than usual this week, and twice forgot to open the forge.

npcs.name: Bram
npcs.description: The plaza's blacksmith, broad and quiet. He is hiding that he has quietly sold the forge and means to leave Lanternmere for good before the festival — he can't bear to say goodbye.

appearance.hair: short grey-streaked hair, soot on his brow
appearance.outfit: scorched leather apron over a grey work shirt

goals: leave town quietly without a scene

secrets.text: Bram has secretly sold the forge and plans to leave Lanternmere for good before the festival.

npcs.name: Sable
npcs.role: herbalist and hedge-witch who notices everything
npcs.description: Keeper of the corner herb stall, sharp-eyed and dry. She trades in remedies and in other people's business, and she has noticed Bram packing crates after dark.

appearance.sourceLook: sly hedge-witch herbalist in a hood
appearance.hair: dark hair under a deep green hood
appearance.outfit: a layered green witch's shawl hung with dried herbs

flaws: hoards what she knows for advantage

memories.text: I saw Bram loading crates onto a cart behind the forge after midnight. He's hiding something.

npcs.name: Old Doran
npcs.role: village elder who has seen it all
npcs.description: The eldest neighbour, once the plaza's headman. Gentle and unhurried, fond of small philosophy. His memory wanders, but his read on people rarely does.

appearance.hair: white hair and a long white beard
appearance.outfit: a worn elder's coat with a brass clasp
appearance.silhouette: stooped elder leaning on a cane

npcs.name: Pip
npcs.description: A quick, bright errand-runner of about ten. Pip cannot hold a thought without sharing it — whatever Pip hears, the whole plaza hears within minutes.

appearance.outfit: an oversized patched tunic and worn boots

goals: be the first to tell everyone the news

npcs.name: Wynne
npcs.description: A traveller who arrived a few days ago and has not decided whether to trust the place. Formerly a caravan guard. Watches more than she speaks, and weighs every rumour for what it's worth.

appearance.hair: black hair in a tight braid
appearance.outfit: a travel-worn soldier's coat with a buckled belt

goals: decide whether Lanternmere is worth staying in
figure out who here is honest
